description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

Saitama Prefecture will start the 'Hazard Map by Ear' service on May 1, 2026 (Reiwa 8). This service is available to people with visual impairments and others, and provides audio information about hazard maps for flooding, landslides, and other disasters.

【Main Features】
• Audio readout of elevation and disaster risks (flood depth, time until water recedes, etc.) for any location like your current position or home
• Voice and vibration guidance to the nearest evacuation site
• Audio readout of weather alerts and warnings from the Japan Meteorological Agency

【How to Use】
Download the free "Uni-Voice Blind" app. It reads voice codes labeled "Uni-Voice" and is available from each app store.

You can check disaster risks and evacuation sites in advance, which helps enable smooth evacuation.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/somubu/bosaikikikanri/5/13507.html
summary: ℹ️ 'Hazard Map by Ear' service launches May 1, 2026
